text:
Market today: Nothing in today's headlines directly moves tech or semis. No macro catalysts, no Fed news, no chip-sector stories. Your book is trading on its own momentum right now, and that momentum is negative on most names.

Your holdings: Three need eyes. PLTR is down -12.77% and flagged SELL — on margin, that hole gets expensive fast; respect that signal. AVGO is your largest position, down -12.45% and flagged TRIM — size plus loss plus margin is a dangerous combo, trim before it forces you to. SPAL is down -14.48% already; it's a 2x leveraged ETF that decays daily, so dead money turns into a slow bleed — your stop at $19 is close, watch it carefully.

Watch: SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history — tiny moves can be sharp; manage it manually and size it carefully on margin. Also watch SMH ($618, +11.32%) — it's your only real winner; if semis roll over, that's your early warning for the whole book.

Bottom line: You have three bleeding positions on a margin book with no positive catalyst in sight — cut or trim before the market makes the decision for you.
